text.title: Puppeteer Session Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the puppeteer-session-management methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: userDataDir MUST be unique per logical identity, not per run. The same identity reuses the same directory; different identities use different directories. Sharing one userDataDir across identities is forbidden.
rule.rationale: userDataDir persists the full Chromium profile (cookies, storage, cert cache); sharing across identities cross-contaminates sessions. Per-run dirs waste login budget and re-trigger MFA.

rule.statement: Concurrent jobs against the same target MUST run in separate browser.createIncognitoBrowserContext() contexts (one browser, isolated state) OR separate browser instances. Sharing one default context across parallel jobs is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Default context shares cookies/storage; concurrent jobs trample each other's auth and produce inconsistent results.

rule.statement: Explicit cookie save/load via page.cookies + page.setCookie is acceptable only when userDataDir is not viable (e.g., stateless function). Mixing both mechanisms in the same identity is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed mechanisms drift apart: userDataDir cookies and the JSON file diverge after the first reload, producing 'logged in then logged out' behaviour.

rule.statement: Long-lived sessions (>5 minutes) MUST live in a daemonised runner exposed via queue/HTTP. The agent enqueues tasks and reads results; the browser stays warm across calls. Running long sessions from a per-task Bash invocation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Per-task invocations re-launch Chromium each time; cold-start dominates and HSTS/cache benefits are lost.

rule.statement: userDataDir paths MUST be outside the repository AND inside a directory backed by encrypted storage. Committing userDataDir contents to git is forbidden.
rule.rationale: userDataDir holds live session cookies; a commit leaks long-lived credentials.

rule.statement: Each createIncognitoBrowserContext MUST be matched by a context.close(). Leaked contexts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Leaked contexts pin memory and cause the browser to OOM under sustained parallel load.
